title: Kalyug

info.type: Rousing, Spiritual, Epic, Mythic, Emotional Journey, Faith & Redemption, Visually Rich, Indian, Inspirational, Drama, Movie

credits.title: Editing Department
credits.names: Jatin Khowar | Renu Chauhan | Krishan Mohan

synopsis: “Kalyug: Ant Ka Aarambh” is a grand, spiritual, and fantasy drama film that begins after the events of the Mahabharata and explores the rise of Kalyug, the spread of Adharma, and the final hope of Dharma.

In the prosperous and well governed kingdom of King Parikshit, an unseen darkness begins to emerge one that gradually takes hold of the entire human society. This darkness is not merely external, but represents the inner evils within humans such as greed, lust, anger, and moral decay.
The story portrays how a mysterious force known as “Kali” enters the human mind, trapping individuals in the web of their own actions, and slowly establishes the dominance of Kalyug across the Earth.

As the narrative unfolds, even a peaceful and devout village falls under the shadow of this darkness, where faith, devotion, and human values are tested against violence, betrayal, and sin. Every character is engaged in an internal battle between Dharma and Adharma.
The film also highlights how human weaknesses become their greatest enemies, and how an unseen force manipulates them to push society toward destruction.
Yet, amidst this growing darkness, a ray of hope emerges the promise of a divine force that will one day descend to restore Dharma once again.
“Kalyug: Ant Ka Aarambh” is not just a story, but a powerful cinematic representation of the truth of Kalyug, human psychology, and the prophecy of Lord Vishnu’s Kalki Avatar.

reviews.name: Aarav Sharma
reviews.text: Damn, that trailer was intense. It really builds the hype, and now I’m genuinely excited to see how the full story unfolds.

reviews.name: Saanvi Gupta
reviews.text: The trailer grabbed my attention instantly. Looks like it’ll have a lot of emotional ups and downs.

reviews.name: Aditya Mehta
reviews.text: Really impressed by the visuals. The trailer has some strong, intense scenes that make it exciting to watch.

reviews.name: Ritik Sharma
reviews.text: That trailer got me interested. It feels more focused on the story and emotions, and it actually leaves you wanting to know more.

reviews.name: Rahul Mishra
reviews.text: It’s one of those trailers that quietly pulls you in. Looks like the story and emotions will take the lead here.

reviews.name: Ajay Mishra
reviews.text: Engaging trailer with strong vibes. Looks promising and worth watching!

reviews.name: Diya Kapoor
reviews.text: Damn, this looks so good! The trailer had me hooked right away. Definitely watching this on day one.
